steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — To compare Spanish-language newspapers published in Texas today with ones published there during the late 1800s: The text never mentions present-day newspapers; it stays entirely in the late 1800s. ✗
  • Choice B — To explain that Spanish-language newspapers thrived in Texas and especially in El Paso during the late 1800s: The text opens with newspapers flourishing across Texas, then highlights El Paso's 22 papers as the peak example, and closes by explaining why. This matches the full arc of the passage. ✓
  • Choice C — To argue that Spanish-language newspapers published in El Paso influenced the ones published in San Antonio during the late 1800s: No causal link between cities is ever made in the text. ✗
  • Choice D — To explain why Spanish-language newspapers published in Texas were so popular in Mexico during the late 1800s: Mexico is mentioned only as a geographic neighbor; readership in Mexico is never discussed. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The matching choice captures both parts of the passage — widespread flourishing across Texas and El Paso's leadership — without adding claims the text never makes. B is correct.
